Off Calendar Nov-28-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Attys. MARK R. KIRKLAND, ESQ. and RONALD KONINI, ESQ. of Kasdan Lippsmith Weber Turner, LLP appearing for plaintiff 55-55A-57-57A-59-59A-61-63-63A Woodward Street TIC;
Atty. COURTNEY H. O'BRIEN, ESQ. of Bishop/Barry (Tel: 510-596-0888) appearing for defendant 55 Woodward, LLC and Magnate Fund #2, LLC;
Atty. GLENN H. WECHSLER, ESQ. of Law Offices of Glenn H. Wechsler appearing via CourtCall for defendant 55 Woodward, LLC;
Atty. WELSEY A. WONG, ESQ. of Wilson, Elser, Moskowitz, Edelman & Dicker LLP (Tel: 415-433-0990) appearing for defendants Magnate Fund #2, LLC and LHJS Investment LLC;
Atty. BENJAMIN W. BLAISDELL, ESQ. of Law Offices of John A. Biard (Tel: 916-638-6621) appearing for LHJS Investments, LLC.

The Court required counsel for parties to appear. The matter is argued and submitted by counsel for parties. The Court adopt its prior tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant LHJS Investment LLC's motion for summary judgment or alternatively summary adjudication on the claims alleged against it in the third amended complaint is denied in its entirety. LHJS fails to maintain its burden because it does not address the alter ego allegations against it. There is also a triable issue whether LHJS negligently converted the premises per Orange Grove Terrace Owners Ass'n v. Bryant Properties, Inc. (1986) 176 Cal.App.3d 1217. Mr. Simonse declares that LHJS performed no contractor or developer activities. Plaintiff presents sufficient evidence to create a triable issue of fact regarding that issue. Contractors made requests for payment directly from LHJS, who is listed as the owner of the project. (Plaintiff's Ex. 4, 00006.) Ronald Paz acted as LJHS's construction manager and he approved Lehane's bills. (Plaintiff's Ex. 4, 00006; Ex. 5, 000002.) The managing member of LHJS (John Simonse) sent an e-mail criticizing and directing the work. (Plaintiff's Ex. 6, 001.)

FORMAL ORDER SIGNED.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant 55 Woodward Llcs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-28-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Attys. MARK R. KIRKLAND, ESQ. and RONALD KONINI, ESQ. of Kasdan Lippsmith Weber Turner, LLP appearing for plaintiff 55-55A-57-57A-59-59A-61-63-63A Woodward Street TIC;
Atty. COURTNEY H. O'BRIEN, ESQ. of Bishop/Barry (Tel: 510-596-0888) appearing for defendant 55 Woodward, LLC and Magnate Fund #2, LLC;
Atty. GLENN H. WECHSLER, ESQ. of Law Offices of Glenn H. Wechsler appearing via CourtCall for defendant 55 Woodward, LLC;
Atty. WELSEY A. WONG, ESQ. of Wilson, Elser, Moskowitz, Edelman & Dicker LLP (Tel: 415-433-0990) appearing for defendants Magnate Fund #2, LLC and LHJS Investment LLC;
Atty. BENJAMIN W. BLAISDELL, ESQ. of Law Offices of John A. Biard (Tel: 916-638-6621) appearing for LHJS Investments, LLC.

The Court required counsel for parties to appear. The matter is argued and submitted by counsel for parties. The Court adopt its prior tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant 55 Woodward, LLC's motion for summary judgment or alternatively summary adjudication on the claims alleged against it in the third amended complaint is denied in its entirety. 55 Woodward fails to demonstrate that it cannot be liable for the negligent conversion of the property per Orange Grove Terrace Owners Ass'n v. Bryant Properties, Inc. (1986) 176 Cal.App.3d 1217. 55 Woodward does not sufficiently address plaintiff's allegations of alter ego liability and that 55 Woodward, Magnate, and LHJS negligently renovated the project and caused damage to plaintiffs. Plaintiff, a TIC, has standing to maintain a claim for breach of contract. (CCP 382 [representative capacity]; Raven's Cove Townhomes, Inc. v. Knuppe Development Co. (1981) 114 Cal.App.3d 783; Market Lofts Community Association v. 9th Street Market Lofts, LLC (2014) 222 Cal.App.4th 924, 932.) Lastly, there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the claims are time barred per CCP 338 as Mr. Paz was remedying problems on a punch list through at least 8/24/12, which was three years before plaintiff filed the action. (Plaintiff's Ex. 5.) The court overrules 55 Woodward's objection to the declaration of counsel Mr. Konini as he has knowledge regarding the attached documents through his participation in this litigation.

FORMAL ORDER SIGNED.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-28-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Attys. MARK R. KIRKLAND, ESQ. and RONALD KONINI, ESQ. of Kasdan Lippsmith Weber Turner, LLP appearing for plaintiff 55-55A-57-57A-59-59A-61-63-63A Woodward Street TIC;
Atty. COURTNEY H. O'BRIEN, ESQ. of Bishop/Barry (Tel: 510-596-0888) appearing for defendant 55 Woodward, LLC and Magnate Fund #2, LLC;
Atty. GLENN H. WECHSLER, ESQ. of Law Offices of Glenn H. Wechsler appearing via CourtCall for defendant 55 Woodward, LLC;
Atty. WELSEY A. WONG, ESQ. of Wilson, Elser, Moskowitz, Edelman & Dicker LLP (Tel: 415-433-0990) appearing for defendants Magnate Fund #2, LLC and LHJS Investment LLC;
Atty. BENJAMIN W. BLAISDELL, ESQ. of Law Offices of John A. Biard (Tel: 916-638-6621) appearing for LHJS Investments, LLC.

The Court required counsel for parties to appear. The matter is argued and submitted by counsel for parties. The Court adopt its prior tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT MAGNATE FUND #2 LLC's Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Defendant Magnate Fund #2, LLC and LHJS Investments, LLC's motion for summary judgment or alternatively summary adjudication on the claims alleged against them in the third amended complaint is denied in its entirety. There is a triable issue whether Magnate and LHJS acted in a conventional role as a mere lender of money or whether they actively participated in the project such that they can be liable for negligence per Daniels v. Select Portfolio Servicing, Inc. (2016) 246 Cal.App.4th 1150, 1181. There is also a triable issue regarding alter ego liability. Movants present evidence that they acted as lenders and there is a separate identity among the entities. (Simonse dec., pars. 2-13.) Plaintiff's controverting evidence creates triable issues of material fact. The evidence discloses that LHJS is the manager of Magnate and 55 Woodward. (Defendants' UMF 4, Simonse Dec., par. 14.) Contractors made requests for payment directly from LHJS, who is listed as the owner of the project. (Plaintiff's Ex. 4, 00006.) The managing member of LHJS (John Simonse) sent an e-mail criticizing and directing the work. (Plaintiff's Ex. 6, 001.) Ronald Paz acted as LJHS's construction manager and he approved Lehane's bills (Plaintiff's Ex. 4, 00006) and coordinated the work (Plaintiff's Ex. 21, 003, 005, 007, 015, 0021, 0068.) He even weighed in on whether to change the paint color of the project. (Plaintiff's Ex. 21, 005.) Magnate acquired title on 6/18/10 (Plaintiff's Ex. 14, 003537) and transferred title to 55 Woodward on 6/22/10 (Plaintiff's Ex. 15, 003542). No transfer tax was paid. (Plaintiff's Ex. 15, 003542.) Prior to transferring title, Magnate hired Vierra Moore to prepare TIC documents. (Plaintiff's Ex. 16, 000093.) Simonse (on behalf of 55 Woodward, Magnate, and LHJS) submitted a supplemental questionnaire to the SFDRE regarding converting the property to a TIC. (Plaintiff's Ex. 2, 000128-129.) 55 Woodward and Magnate never had any employees. (Plaintiff's Ex.17, Simonse Depo., 21:9-11.) Magnate, 55 Woodward, and LHJS all shared the same address. (Plaintiff's Ex. 19.) David Waite is managing member of LHJS and had authority to act for Magnate with regard to selling properties. (Plaintiff's Ex. 9, 003536.) There is no information about the amount or type of insurance that 55 Woodward obtained. (Defendants' UMF 15.) 55 Woodward no longer exists. (Defendants' UMF 20.) A reasonable trier of fact could conclude that defendants were actively involved in the construction project, there is a unity of interest among the parties, and respecting the corporate/LLC form could lead to an inequitable result.

FORMAL ORDER SIGNED.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-15-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT MAGNATE FUND #2 LLC'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with twenty days leave to amend. As to the strict liability and negligence causes of action, while paragraph 3b alleges that Magnate transferred the property and the conversion was ongoing when Woodward took title, the FAC does not clearly allege that Magnate constructed or renovated the properties which could create liability under Kriegler v. Eichler Homes, Inc. (1969) 269 Cal.App.2d 224 (products liability claim against mass producer of homes). As to the breach of implied warranty and breach of contract causes of action, plaintiff fails to attach copies of the contracts, plead their terms verbatim, or plead the contracts according to their legal intendment and effect. Negligence per se is not an independent cause of action and plaintiff has not alleged the statutory violations in the negligence claim. (Sierra-Bay Fed. Land Bank Assn. v. Sup. Crt. (1991) 227 Cal.App.3d 318, 333). The FAC does not show on its face that any of the claims are necessarily time-barred and paragraph 3b alleges that the damages were discovered within 3 years of filing the complaint. Order is signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/hk)
